title: Core Components Overview
description: Meet the essential building blocks: View, Text, Image, and more.

slides.title: What Are Core Components? 🧱
slides.content:
# Components that map to native UI React Native provides a set of **core components** that are translated into native widgets. Here are the most common ones: | Component | iOS | Android | Web analog | |-----------|-----|---------|------------| | `` | UIView | android.View | `
` | | `` | UILabel | android.widget.TextView | `

` / `` | | `` | UIImageView | android.widget.ImageView | `` | | `` | UIScrollView | android.widget.ScrollView | `

` with scrolling | | `` | UITextField | android.widget.EditText | `` / `

slides.title: Using Core Components
slides.content: Here's a simple screen with several core components:

slides.title: Important Props 🏷️
slides.content:
## Common Props

* **`style`** – every component accepts a style object or array.
* **`onPress`** – for touchable components (Button, TouchableOpacity, etc.).
* **`source`** – for Image, specifies the image (local or remote).
* **`placeholder`** – for TextInput, hint text.
* **`value`** – for TextInput, controlled input.

quizData.question: Which core component would you use to display a paragraph of text?
quizData.explanation: `<Text>` is the only component specifically for displaying text.
